title: Find City With Fewest Neighbors Within Threshold
description:
Find There are n cities labeled 0..n-1 with weighted undirected edges. For a distance threshold T, find the city with the smallest number of other cities that are reachable within T by shortest paths. Break ties by returning the city with the largest index. Use Floyd–Warshall for all-pairs shortest paths.

    

Constraints:
    - 2 ≤ n ≤ 100
- 0 ≤ |edges| ≤ n(n-1)/2
- Weights nonnegative
explanation:
Intuition
Compute all-pairs distances, then count how many are ≤ T for each city. Floyd–Warshall is straightforward and fits constraints.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize dist with 0 on diagonal and edge weights elsewhere
2. Main Algorithm: Run triple loop updating dist[i][j] = min(dist[i][j], dist[i][k]+dist[k][j])
3. Return Result: Scan counts and apply tie-breaking

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^3)
- Space Complexity: O(n^2)

Key Insights
- Tie-breaking favors larger index on equal counts
- Initialize missing edges to INF

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting symmetric assignment for undirected edges
- Overflow if INF not large enough

testCases.explanation: City 3 ties with 0 but larger index
Both have 0 neighbors, pick larger index
All reach all; tie broken by largest index

hints: Initialize dist with INF and 0 on diagonal
Run Floyd–Warshall to get all shortest paths
Count neighbors ≤ threshold and break ties by index
